expectations.text: Output uses the worker handoff summary format (not the implementation or closeout format)
expectations.evidence: Header reads '## Worker Handoff Summary' and follows Format 2 fields.

expectations.text: All 6 fields appear: Task (or Bead), Branch with Published+Commit, Implementation Outcome with integration target, Quality Gates, Risks/Gaps, Review Notes
expectations.evidence: Bead, Branch (with Published and Commit handed off), Implementation Outcome (with Intended integration target), Quality Gates, Risks/Gaps, and Review Notes all appear as labeled fields.

expectations.text: Branch field names feature/proj-42-w2_staging-source-ref, marks Published as yes, includes commit SHA 7a3f9b1
expectations.evidence: 'Branch: feature/proj-42-w2_staging-source-ref / Published: yes / Commit handed off: 7a3f9b1'.

expectations.text: Quality Gates section reports concrete results (142/142 unit tests, 0 lint errors, typecheck passed) — not vague
expectations.evidence: 'Lint: passed (0 errors); Typecheck: passed; Unit tests: 142/142 passing'.

expectations.text: Review Notes call out the github.head_ref consideration the worker wants the orchestrator to verify
expectations.evidence: Review Notes describes the github.head_ref behavior on PR vs direct-push events and asks the orchestrator to confirm the parent-epic protection assumption.

expectations.text: Output is concise (worker handoff is shorter than the user-facing implementation summary; no Behavioral Impact / File Reference Summary unless explicitly relevant)
expectations.evidence: No Behavioral Impact or File Reference Summary sections are included.

expectations.text: Output does not falsely claim the branch was accepted, merged, or that closeout happened — handoff is pre-acceptance
expectations.evidence: Branch is 'Published: yes' but no acceptance/merge/closeout language; framed as a handoff for the orchestrator to confirm.

expectations.text: Output does not cite this skill by name
expectations.evidence: No mention of the skill name or self-reference.
